items.name: Database
items.description: ORM entry point.  Provides the `Database` factory that creates a connection to a backing store, the base `Model` class, the `TYPES` enum, and schema helpers. Supported adapters (all optional "bring your own driver"): - `memory`  — in-process (no driver needed) - `json`    — JSON file persistence (no driver needed) - `sqlite`  — requires `better-sqlite3` - `mysql`   — requires `mysql2` - `postgres` — requires `pg` - `mongo`   — requires `mongodb`

methods.description: Create a Database connection.

methodParams.notes: Adapter type: memory, json, sqlite, mysql, postgres, mongo.

methods.description: Register a Model class with this database. Binds the adapter to the model so all CRUD operations go through it.
Register multiple Model classes at once.
Synchronise all registered models — create tables if they don't exist. Tables are ordered so referenced tables are created first (topological sort).
Drop all registered model tables (in reverse order to respect FK deps).
Close the underlying connection / pool.
Get a registered model by table name.

methodReturns.description: The registered Model class, or undefined.

methods.description: Execute a callback within a database transaction. If the callback throws, the transaction is rolled back. If the callback returns normally, the transaction is committed. Note: Transaction support depends on the adapter. Memory and JSON adapters run the callback directly (no real transaction).

methodParams.notes: Async callback to execute within the transaction.

methodReturns.description: The return value of the callback.

methods.description: Add a column to an existing table.
Drop a column from a table.
Rename a column.
Rename a table.
Create an index on a table.
Drop an index.
Check if a table exists.
Check if a column exists on a table.
Get detailed column info for a table.

methodReturns.description: Column definition objects for the table.

methods.description: Add a foreign key constraint (MySQL / PostgreSQL only).

methodOptions.notes: Referential action on delete (CASCADE, SET NULL, RESTRICT, etc.).
Referential action on update (CASCADE, SET NULL, RESTRICT, etc.).

methods.description: Drop a foreign key constraint (MySQL / PostgreSQL only).
Ping the database to check connectivity. Works across all adapters.
Execute a function with automatic retry on failure. Uses exponential backoff with jitter.
Enable query profiling on this database. Attaches a QueryProfiler that tracks every query execution, detects slow queries, and flags potential N+1 patterns.

methodOptions.notes: Duration (ms) above which a query is considered slow.
Minimum rapid SELECTs to flag N+1.

methods.description: The attached profiler (if profiling is enabled).
The attached replica manager (if configured).
Create a Database with read replica support. Automatically sets up a ReplicaManager with the primary and replica adapters.

methodParams.notes: ] - Array of options for each replica adapter.

methodOptions.notes: Read from primary after a write.

items.name: Model
items.description: Base Model class for defining database-backed entities. Provides static CRUD methods, instance-level save/update/delete, lifecycle hooks, relationship definitions, computed/virtual columns, attribute casting, model events & observers, and advanced relationships.

methods.description: Save this instance to the database. Insert if new, update if persisted.
Update specific fields on this instance.
Delete this instance from the database. If softDelete is enabled, sets deletedAt instead.
Restore a soft-deleted record.
Increment a numeric field atomically.
Decrement a numeric field atomically.
Reload this instance from the database.
Convert to plain object (for JSON serialization). Respects `static hidden = [...]` to exclude sensitive fields. Includes computed columns and applies accessor transformations.

methodReturns.description: Plain data object with hidden fields excluded.

methods.description: Create and persist a new record.
Create multiple records at once. Uses batch INSERT when the adapter supports it (much faster for SQL databases).
Find records matching conditions.
Find a single record matching conditions.
Find a record by primary key.
Find one or create if not found.
Update records matching conditions.
Delete records matching conditions.
Count records matching conditions.
Check whether any records matching conditions exist.

methodReturns.description: True if any matching records exist.

methods.description: Insert or update a record matching conditions. If a matching record exists, update it. Otherwise, create a new one.

methodParams.notes: Data to set (merged with conditions on create).

methods.description: Start a query with a named scope applied.

methodParams.notes: Additional arguments passed to the scope function.

methods.description: Start a fluent query builder.
Find the first record matching optional conditions.
Find the last record matching optional conditions.
Rich pagination with metadata. Returns `{ data, total, page, perPage, pages, hasNext, hasPrev }`.

methodReturns.description: Pagination result with data, total, page, perPage, pages, hasNext, hasPrev.

methods.description: Process all matching records in batches. Calls `fn(batch, batchIndex)` for each chunk.
Get all records, optionally filtered. Alias for find() — for LINQ-familiarity.
Get a random record.
Pluck values for a single column across all matching records.
Define a hasMany relationship.

methodParams.notes: Foreign key column on the related table.

methods.description: Define a hasOne relationship.

methodParams.notes: Foreign key column on the related table.

methods.description: Define a belongsTo relationship.

methodParams.notes: Foreign key column on THIS table.
Key on the related table (default: its primary key).

methods.description: Define a many-to-many relationship through a junction/pivot table.

methodOptions.notes: Column on the junction table referencing THIS model.
Column on the junction table referencing the related model.
Related model key (default: its primary key).

methods.description: Load a related model for this instance.

methodParams.notes: Name of the related Model class or relation alias.

methods.description: Get an attribute value with accessor/cast applied.
Set an attribute value with mutator/cast applied.
Register an event listener on this model. Supported events: `creating`, `created`, `updating`, `updated`, `deleting`, `deleted`, `saving`, `saved`.
Register a one-time event listener.
Remove an event listener.
Remove all listeners for an event, or all listeners entirely.

methodParams.notes: Event name. If omitted, removes all listeners.

methods.description: Register an observer for this model. An observer is an object with methods named after lifecycle events: `creating`, `created`, `updating`, `updated`, `deleting`, `deleted`.

methodParams.notes: Observer object with event handler methods.

methods.description: Remove an observer from this model.
Define a polymorphic one-to-one relationship (morphOne). The related table uses two columns: a type column and an ID column.

methodParams.notes: Base name for the polymorphic columns (e.g. 'commentable').

methods.description: Define a polymorphic one-to-many relationship (morphMany). The related table uses two columns: a type column and an ID column.

methodParams.notes: Base name for the polymorphic columns (e.g. 'commentable').

methods.description: Define a has-many-through relationship. Accesses distant relations through an intermediate table.

methodParams.notes: FK on the through table referencing this model.
FK on the related table referencing the through table.
Key on the through table matched by secondKey (default: through model PK).

methods.description: Define a self-referential relationship for tree/graph structures. Sets up both parent and children relationships.
Build a full tree structure from self-referential records. Returns nested objects with a `children` array property.

methodOptions.notes: FK column for the parent reference.
Value of foreignKey that indicates a root node.

methodReturns.description: Array of root nodes with nested children.

methods.description: Get all ancestors of this instance in a self-referential tree.

methodParams.notes: FK column for the parent reference.

methodReturns.description: Array of ancestors from immediate parent to root.

methods.description: Get all descendants of this instance in a self-referential tree.

methodParams.notes: FK column for the parent reference.

items.name: Schema DDL
items.description: Schema definition and validation for ORM models. Validates data against column definitions, coerces types, and enforces constraints (required, unique, min, max, enum, match).

methods.description: Validate all columns of a data object against the schema.

methodOptions.notes: When true, only validates provided fields (for updates).

methodReturns.type: { valid: boolean, errors: string[], sanitized: object }

methods.description: Validate and return a FK action string, or throw.

methodParams.notes: Foreign key action string (CASCADE, SET NULL, etc.).

methods.description: Validate a CHECK expression for dangerous SQL patterns.

items.name: TYPES
items.description: Column type constants for ORM schema definitions. Use these when defining model schemas to specify the data type of each column.

items.name: Query
items.description: Fluent query builder that produces adapter-agnostic query objects. Each method returns `this` for chaining.  Call `.exec()` or `await` the query to execute it against the adapter.
items.example:
const users = await User.query()
      .where('age', '>', 18)
      .where('role', 'admin')
      .orderBy('name', 'asc')
      .limit(10)
      .offset(20)
      .select('name', 'email');

methods.description: Select specific columns.
Select distinct rows.
Add a WHERE condition. Accepts multiple forms: - `where('age', 18)` → `age = 18` - `where('age', '>', 18)` → `age > 18` - `where({ role: 'admin', active: true })` → `role = 'admin' AND active = true`

methodParams.notes: Operator (=, !=, >, <, >=, <=, LIKE, IN, NOT IN, BETWEEN, IS NULL, IS NOT NULL).

methods.description: Add an OR WHERE condition.
WHERE column IS NULL.
WHERE column IS NOT NULL.
WHERE column IN (...values).
WHERE column NOT IN (...values).
WHERE column BETWEEN low AND high.
WHERE column NOT BETWEEN low AND high.
WHERE column LIKE pattern.
ORDER BY a column.
Add secondary sort ascending (use after orderBy).
Add secondary sort descending (use after orderBy).
LIMIT results.
OFFSET results.
Convenience: page(pageNum, perPage).
GROUP BY column(s).
HAVING (used with GROUP BY).
INNER JOIN.
LEFT JOIN.
RIGHT JOIN.
Include soft-deleted records in results.
Eager-load one or more relationships. Batches related queries to avoid the N+1 problem. Accepts either relation names or a relation name + a scope function to constrain the sub-query.

methodParams.notes: Relation names or `{ RelationName: q => q.where(...) }`.

methods.description: Alias for with() — mirrors Entity Framework include syntax.

methodParams.notes: Relation names or config objects to eager-load.

methods.description: Eager-count one or more relationships without loading the records. Adds a `RelationName_count` field to each result instance.
Force this query to execute against a read replica (if configured). Falls back to primary adapter if no replica manager is attached.
Get the query execution plan from the adapter. For SQL adapters, returns EXPLAIN / EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN output. For the memory adapter, returns a plan description object.
Apply a named scope from the model. Allows chaining multiple scopes on a single query.

methodParams.notes: Additional arguments for the scope function.

methods.description: Build the abstract query descriptor.
Execute the query and return results.
Execute and return the first result.
Count matching records.
Check whether any matching records exist.

methodReturns.description: True if any matching records exist.

methods.description: Get an array of values for a single column.
SUM of a numeric column.
AVG of a numeric column.
MIN of a column.
MAX of a column.
Make Query thenable — allows `await query`.
Alias for limit (LINQ naming).
Alias for offset (LINQ naming).
Alias for exec — explicitly convert to array.
Shorthand for orderBy(field, 'desc').
C# alias: OrderByDescending.
Alias for first() — C# FirstOrDefault returns null on empty.
Alias for avg() — C# naming.
Alias for reduce() — C# Aggregate naming.
Execute and return the last result. Reverses the first orderBy or defaults to primary key DESC.
Alias for last() — C# naming.
Returns the only element. Throws if count !== 1.
Returns the only element, or null if empty. Throws if more than one.
Get element at a specific index.
Get element at index, or null if out of range.
Returns results, or an array with defaultValue if empty.
Returns true if any elements match. With a predicate, filters post-execution. Without a predicate, equivalent to exists().
Returns true if all elements match the predicate.

methodReturns.description: True if all elements match the predicate.

methods.description: Returns true if any record has the given value for a column.

methodReturns.description: True if any record has the given value.

methods.description: Compares results of this query with another for equality.

methodReturns.description: True if both sequences are equal.

methods.description: Append results from another query or array.

methodParams.notes: Query or array to combine with.

methods.description: Distinct union of this query's results with another.

methodParams.notes: Query or array to combine with.

methods.description: Elements common to both this query and another.

methodParams.notes: Query or array to combine with.

methods.description: Elements in this query but not in other.

methodParams.notes: Query or array to combine with.

methods.description: FlatMap — project each element to an array and flatten.
Combine two result sets element-wise.

methodParams.notes: Query or array to combine with.

methods.description: Convert results to a Map keyed by a selector.

methodParams.notes: (item) => value. Defaults to the item itself.

methods.description: Group results into a Map of arrays keyed by a selector.
Take elements while predicate returns true (post-execution).
Skip elements while predicate returns true, then return the rest.

methodReturns.description: Remaining elements after the predicate stops matching.

methods.description: Reverse the result order.
Append items to the end of results.
Prepend items to the beginning of results.
Distinct by a key selector (post-execution).
Element with the minimum value from a selector.
Element with the maximum value from a selector.
Sum using a value selector.
Average using a value selector.
Count elements per group using a key selector.
Conditionally apply query logic. If `condition` is truthy, calls `fn(query)`. Perfect for optional filters.
Inverse of when — apply query logic when condition is falsy.
Inspect the query without breaking the chain. Calls `fn(this)` for side effects (logging, debugging).
Process results in batches. Calls `fn(batch, batchIndex)` for each chunk. Useful for processing large datasets without loading everything into memory.
Execute and iterate each result with a callback.
Execute, transform results with a mapper, and return the mapped array.

methodParams.notes: Called with (item, index). Return the mapped value.

Rich pagination with metadata. Returns `{ data, total, page, perPage, pages, hasNext, hasPrev }`.

methodReturns.description: Pagination result with data, total, page, perPage, pages, hasNext, hasPrev.

methods.description: Inject a raw WHERE clause for SQL adapters. Ignored by non-SQL adapters (memory, json, mongo).

items.name: Redis Adapter
items.description: Redis database adapter for the zero-http ORM. Uses `ioredis` as the driver. Stores table data as Redis hashes with sorted-set indexes for ordering and filtering. Bring-your-own-driver: `npm install ioredis` Supports full ORM CRUD, key-value operations, pub/sub, pipelines, TTL, and all DDL/migration methods.

options.notes: If true, defer connection until first operation.

methods.description: Create a table (register schema + initialize index).
Drop a table (delete all keys).
Insert a row.
Insert multiple rows.
Update a row by primary key.
Update all rows matching conditions.
Remove a row by primary key.
Delete all rows matching conditions.
Execute a query descriptor (from the Query builder).

methodReturns.description: Matching rows, or count if action is `'count'`.

Get a value by key (raw Redis GET).

methodReturns.description: Stored value, or `null` if not found.

methods.description: Set a key-value pair (raw Redis SET).
Delete a key.
Check if a key exists.
Set expiration on a key.

methodReturns.description: `1` if timeout was set, `0` if key does not exist.

items.name: Memory Adapter
items.description: In-memory database adapter. Zero-dependency, perfect for testing, prototyping, and applications that don't need persistence beyond the process lifecycle. All data is stored in plain JavaScript Maps and arrays. Supports full CRUD, filtering, ordering, pagination, and counting.

items.name: FullTextSearch
items.description: Full-text search integration for the ORM. Provides a unified API across PostgreSQL (tsvector/tsquery), MySQL (FULLTEXT), SQLite (FTS5), and in-memory (regex-based).

items.name: GeoQuery
items.description: Geo-spatial query support for the ORM. Provides distance calculations, bounding box queries, radius searches, and GeoJSON support. Works with in-memory adapters using Haversine formula; SQL adapters can use native spatial extensions (PostGIS, MySQL spatial).

items.name: TenantManager

items.name: CLI
items.description: CLI tool for zero-http ORM operations. Provides commands for migrations, seeding, and scaffolding. Requires a `zero.config.js` (or `.zero-http.js`) in your project root that exports your database adapter and connection settings.

commandGroups.label: Scaffolding
commandGroups.description: Generate starter files so you don't write boilerplate by hand. Every file is placed in the directory set by your config (or overridden with --dir).

commands.desc: Auto-generate a migration by comparing your current Model schemas against the last snapshot. The CLI writes the up() and down() code for you — no manual SQL. Pass --empty to get a blank template instead.
Create a new Model class with a table name, primary key, and timestamps enabled.
Create a new Seeder class for inserting sample or default data.

commandGroups.label: Seeding
commandGroups.description: Populate your database with initial or test data. Seeders run in alphabetical file order.

commands.desc: Run every seeder file in the seeders directory.

commandGroups.label: Running Migrations
commandGroups.description: Apply, rollback, and inspect versioned migration files. Each run is tracked in a _migrations table so every environment stays in sync.

commands.desc: Run all pending migrations in order. Tracks each run in a numbered batch for easy rollback.
Undo the last batch of migrations. Calls each migration's down() in reverse order.
Show which migrations have been applied and which are still pending.
Rollback every migration, then re-run them all from the start.
Drop all tables and re-run every migration from scratch. Useful during early development.
Delete the most recent migration file — but only if it hasn't been applied yet. Also reverts the schema snapshot.
Print the full help message with all commands and options.

cliOptions.desc: Models directory for auto-diff migrations (default: modelsDir from config or "models").
Generate a blank migration template (skip auto-diff).

workflows.description: Set up your project, create models, and let the CLI auto-generate migrations from your schemas.

steps.label: 1. Create a config file
2. Create your models
steps.note: Scaffold new model files with the CLI, or point modelsDir at models you already have.

tabs.code:
$ npx zh make:model User
$ npx zh make:model Post

steps.label: 3. Define your schemas
4. Auto-generate the migration
steps.code:
$ npx zh make:migration initial_schema

  Detected schema changes:
    + Table users

  Migration created: migrations/20260402120000_initial_schema.js

steps.label: 5. Apply the migration
steps.code:
$ npx zh migrate

  Running migrations...
    ✓ 20260402120000_initial_schema

  1 migration(s) completed (batch 1).

steps.label: 6. Later — add a column
steps.code:
// Add to models/User.js schema:
//   avatar: { type: TYPES.STRING, maxLength: 255 },

$ npx zh make:migration add_avatar_to_users

  Detected schema changes:
    + users.avatar

  Migration created: migrations/20260403090000_add_avatar_to_users.js

$ npx zh migrate

workflows.description: Populate your database with sample or default data. Seeders run alphabetically — use numeric prefixes to control the order.

steps.label: 2. Write your seed data
3. Run your seeders
steps.code:
$ npx zh seed

  Running seeders...
    ✓ UsersSeeder

  1 seeder(s) completed.

workflows.description: Roll back mistakes, reset your schema, or start fresh during development.

steps.label: Rollback the last batch
steps.code:
$ npx zh migrate:rollback

  Rolling back batch 2...
    ✓ 20260403090000_add_avatar_to_users

  1 migration(s) rolled back.

steps.label: Remove an unapplied migration
steps.code:
$ npx zh migrate:remove

  Removed: 20260403090000_add_avatar_to_users.js
  Snapshot updated.

steps.label: Start completely fresh
steps.code:
// Drop all tables and re-run every migration
$ npx zh migrate:fresh

  Dropped all tables.
  Running migrations...
    ✓ 20260402120000_initial_schema

  1 migration(s) completed (batch 1).
